race.name: UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake
race.display_name: UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake
race.tagline: China's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series on the scenic 2022 Asian Games course at Thousand Island Lake

vitals.location: Qiandao Lake, Chun’an County, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China

climate.description: Mild subtropical (15-25°C / 59-77°F in May), possible rain
challenges: Mild subtropical (15-25°C / 59-77°F in May), possible rain

terrain.surface: Mostly flat with elevation 100-326m; three categorized climbs: Cat4 (2.59km/3.7% @11km), Cat3 (3km/5.2% @30km), Cat4 (1.73km/6% @48km); well-paved lakeside roads through villages and countryside

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 73. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 71.

course_description.character: 107km Gran Fondo starts north Qiandao Lake, big counterclockwise loop through lakeside roads, quiet villages, lush countryside. **Suffering zones**: Cat4 2.59km/3.7% (km11), Cat3 3km/5.2% (km30), Cat4 1.73km/6% (km48). Feed zone km60. Flat/fast 21km TT loop in Liangzhu (11m elevation).

logistics.transport: Hangzhou Xiaoshan Intl (HGH) ~2-3hr drive. Time trial in Liangzhu (30min NW Hangzhou). North side Qiandao Lake start/finish. International field support expected.

history.origin_story: Founded 2025 as China's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series event and first international A-class cycling in Hangzhou post-2023 Asian Games. 2025 debut (Sep 6-7) drew 1,500+ riders from 30 countries as final qualifier for UCI World Championships. 2026 edition May 23-24 on 2022 Asian Games course.

biased_opinion.summary: Historic first Chinese UCI Gran Fondo at stunning Qiandao Lake (1,078 islands). 2026's punchier 107km/1,800m course improves on 2025's flatter 136km. Qualifier prestige + tourist paradise = bucket-list event despite travel costs.
